notes: The idfa-ops skill achieves perfect scores (100%) on 8 of 10 evals, with the only exceptions being write-recalc-read-pattern (83%, due to LibreOffice unavailability forcing manual formula tracing) and sequential-multi-write (0%, due to missing output artifacts).

The most consistent differentiator is tool usage: with_skill runs use idfa_ops.py and idfa_audit.py scripts exclusively, while without_skill runs default to direct openpyxl manipulation. This difference accounts for the majority of expectation failures in without_skill runs.

The sequential-multi-write eval scores 0% for both configurations — with_skill due to missing output artifacts (executor issue), without_skill due to using openpyxl instead of idfa_ops.py. This eval may need investigation for executor reliability.

Without the skill, the agent can still accomplish the core task (reading/writing/inspecting models) but loses IDFA-specific behaviors: structured JSON output, guardrail-based auditing, and the write-recalc-read pattern discipline.

The audit evals show the largest gap: with_skill produces IDFA-standard reports with all 4 guardrails, compliance scores, and remediation plans; without_skill produces generic quality assessments that miss LaTeX verification (G2) and intent notes (G3) entirely.
